title: Networks including the internet
summary: 9618 AS — LAN/WAN topologies, TCP/IP layers, routing, DNS, and client-server vs peer-to-peer models.
learningObjectives: Compare LAN, WAN, and WLAN with examples.
Describe star, bus, and mesh topologies.
Explain the role of IP, TCP, routers, and DNS.

simpleExplanation.title: Connected systems
simpleExplanation.summary: Networks link devices to share resources. The internet is a global WAN using packet switching and layered protocols.
steps: Star topology: central switch — one cable failure isolates one node.
Packet switching: data split into packets with headers; routed independently.
TCP: reliable delivery; IP: addressing and routing between networks.
DNS maps domain names (e.g. example.com) to IP addresses.

sections.content: **Networks** (2.1) underpin **security** (6.1) and **web technologies** (A Level). Draw **topology diagrams** and trace a **DNS lookup → TCP connection** sequence.

items: Router forwards packets between networks using routing tables
Cloud services often use client-server over the internet
